facility_name: NVIDIA Eos AI Supercomputer (Voyager Building)
cooling_type: Direct Liquid Cooling (DLC)
opportunity_summary: NVIDIA's flagship internal AI supercomputer, 576 DGX H100 nodes (4,608 GPUs), ranked #9 TOP500, 18.4 exaflops FP8. Liquid cooling rack manifolds in 90,000 sf data labs within Voyager/Endeavor campus buildings.
notes: Eos uses liquid cooling manifolds. Campus also includes waterside and airside economizers. Source: aidatacenterindex.com (35 MW); Taylor Engineers (campus design); TOP500 listing.

facility_name: NVIDIA Mevo Carmel AI Campus
cooling_type: Liquid Cooling (advanced, imported to Israel for this project)
opportunity_summary: 10,000 sqm facility hosting hundreds of liquid-cooled Blackwell GPU systems plus BlueField-3 SuperNIC, Spectrum-X800 switches. $500M–$1.5B investment. Advanced liquid-cooling tech being introduced to Israel specifically for this project.
notes: DCD reports 30MW IT load; Calcalist/eWeek report 64MW total electrical capacity. Liquid cooling explicitly confirmed for Blackwell-based systems. Operations start expected H1 2025/2026. Source: DCD; Calcalist; eWeek; dcpulse.com.

facility_name: JUPITER Exascale AI Supercomputer
cooling_type: Direct Liquid Cooling (Eviden BullSequana XH3000 patented DLC)
opportunity_summary: Europe's first exascale supercomputer. Built on Eviden BullSequana XH3000 liquid-cooled architecture. ~50 container modules on 2,300 sqm. Patented DLC won #1 Green500 ranking. Waste heat recovery feeds campus district heating.
notes: Liquid cooling explicitly confirmed by Eviden and NVIDIA press. Modular containerized design. PUE not publicly stated but Green500 top rank. Source: DCD; Eviden press; NVIDIA blog; Forschungszentrum Jülich.

facility_name: AIST ABCI 3.0 Supercomputer
operator_company: AIST (National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology) / NVIDIA
cooling_type: Direct Liquid Cooling (50kW liquid + 10kW air per rack, 32°C water)
opportunity_summary: Japan's fastest AI supercomputer. HPE Cray XD system with thousands of NVIDIA H200 GPUs. Liquid cooling uses 32°C water, 3MW free cooling tower + 200kW chiller. Built by Fujitsu with Asetek liquid cooling technology.
notes: Liquid cooling explicitly confirmed by Asetek press release and NVIDIA blog. Rack-level: 50kW liquid + 10kW air cooling per rack. Source: NVIDIA blog; Asetek press; glennklockwood.com system profile.

facility_name: TSMC Fab 12B HPC Immersion Cooling Deployment
facility_type: Semiconductor Fab with HPC Immersion Cooling
cooling_type: Immersion Cooling (single-phase, sealed tank with 3M fluid)
opportunity_summary: TSMC deployed immersion cooling in Fab 12B (Jan 2022) for HPC servers. Partners: Delta Electronics, Gigabyte, Accton, MIRLE, 3M. Saves 30% energy, reduces waste 50%, boosts compute 10%. Target: 400M kWh/yr savings by 2030.
notes: This is an HPC data center deployment WITHIN a semiconductor fab, not a standalone DC. Immersion cooling explicitly confirmed in TSMC ESG report. Address: 168 Park Ave. 2, Hsinchu Science Park. Source: TSMC ESG case study.

facility_name: Samsung SDS Dongtan HPC Data Center
cooling_type: Liquid Cooling + Hot Aisle Containment + UCC (Underfloor Close Coupled)
opportunity_summary: South Korea's first HPC-dedicated data center. 114 MW, 116,000 servers, PUE 1.12. Liquid cooling applied from design stage. ORV2 OCP-compliant servers (66kW per rack). Opened Dec 2022. World-class energy efficiency.
notes: Liquid cooling confirmed by Samsung Semiconductor tech blog and DCD reporting. Address: 14 Dongtan-daero 9na-gil, Hwaseong-si, Gyeonggi-do 18488. Source: DCD; Samsung Semiconductor blog; datacentermap.com; baxtel.com.

facility_name: Samsung Hwaseong HPC Center (Semiconductor Division)
facility_type: HPC Data Center for Semiconductor R&D
cooling_type: Underfloor Close Coupled (UCC) + Hot Aisle Containment
opportunity_summary: Samsung Semiconductor's HPC Center targeting PUE 1.15. Features ORV2 OCP servers customized to 66kW per rack. UCC cooling system for maximized efficiency. May overlap with Samsung SDS Dongtan facility.
notes: Samsung Semiconductor blog describes UCC cooling + hot aisle containment but does not explicitly confirm liquid cooling (vs Samsung SDS Dongtan which does confirm liquid cooling). These may be the same or adjacent facilities. Source: Samsung Semiconductor tech blog.

facility_name: Intel Jones Farm Data Center Mega Lab
facility_type: Data Center R&D Lab / Immersion Cooling Showcase
cooling_type: Immersion Liquid Cooling (open-IP reference design)
opportunity_summary: $700M investment, 200,000 sqft R&D lab at Jones Farm campus. Focus: immersion cooling, water usage effectiveness, heat recapture/reuse. Introduced industry's first open-IP immersion liquid cooling reference design. Customer/partner showcase facility.
notes: Immersion cooling explicitly confirmed by Intel, DCD, insideHPC. Construction began 2022, opened ~late 2023. Also tests Intel products in various DC environments. Source: DCD; insideHPC; TechSpot; NetworkWorld.

facility_name: Intel-ITRI Joint Lab for HPC Immersion Cooling
cooling_type: Immersion Cooling (validation and certification lab)
opportunity_summary: Joint lab for HPC cooling certification. Offers comprehensive verification: materials testing, server component compatibility, service life cycle evaluations. Intel's proof-of-concept for scaling immersion cooling globally via Taiwanese ecosystem.
notes: Inaugurated Jan 2024. Immersion cooling explicitly confirmed. Lab validates industrial immersion cooling solutions for HPC. Phased approach with plans to scale globally. Source: ITRI Today; Digitimes; Intel press.

facility_name: Broadcom San Jose Design Labs (Liquid Cooling Upgrade)
facility_type: Semiconductor Design Lab / Internal Data Center
cooling_type: Direct Liquid Cooling (closed-loop overhead piping)
opportunity_summary: Broadcom upgrading internal design labs/DC on San Jose campus to liquid cooling. Closed-loop system pipes water/coolant overhead. Supports switch development and QA. Also partnered with JetCool for direct-to-chip cooling on AI XPUs (4 W/mm2 heat flux).
notes: Liquid cooling upgrade confirmed by TechBrew lab tour (Nov 2025). JetCool collaboration announced Mar 2026 for next-gen AI XPUs. Broadcom campus address not specified precisely. Source: TechBrew; Flex/JetCool press release; Digitimes.

facility_name: Qualcomm-HUMAIN AI Data Center
cooling_type: Direct Liquid Cooling
opportunity_summary: Qualcomm re-entering DC market via HUMAIN partnership. 200MW target in 2026. AI200 and AI250 rack solutions with DLC, 160kW rack-level power, confidential computing. First customer for Qualcomm DC chips.
notes: DLC confirmed by Qualcomm product specs and DCD reporting. AI200 available 2026, AI250 available 2027. Qualcomm also establishing semiconductor design center in Saudi Arabia. Source: Qualcomm press; DCD; DataCenterKnowledge.

facility_name: Samsung C&T Hanam Data Center
cooling_type: Advanced Cooling (GRC immersion cooling partnership for future projects)
opportunity_summary: Samsung C&T completed 40MW DC in Hanam suburb of Seoul (2024). Samsung C&T partnered with GRC (Green Revolution Cooling) for immersion cooling in future projects. Samsung Ventures invested in GRC. Samsung also acquired FläktGroup for 1.5B EUR.
notes: The Hanam facility itself may not yet have immersion cooling - the GRC partnership (June 2025) targets future deployments. FläktGroup acquisition provides liquid cooling product capability. Source: Samsung C&T Newsroom; PRNewswire; Samsung Global Newsroom.

facility_name: Nebius AI Factory (NVIDIA-Powered)
cooling_type: Closed-Loop Liquid Cooling (no local water intake)
opportunity_summary: Mäntsälä has Europe's first NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 deployment. Lappeenranta expansion: 310MW, 100 acres, closed-loop liquid cooling with zero local water intake, heat recovery to district heating. NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 later in 2026. First capacity 2027.
notes: Liquid cooling confirmed by Nebius press (Mar 2026). 700 construction jobs, 100 permanent. Listed under NVIDIA on aidatacenterindex. Source: Nebius Newsroom; BusinessWire; DCD.

facility_name: SoftBank Tomakomai AI Data Center (NVIDIA-Powered)
cooling_type: Liquid Cooling (expected for NVIDIA GB200/Blackwell infrastructure)
opportunity_summary: 300MW AI data center under construction in Tomakomai. Part of SoftBank's massive AI infrastructure push with NVIDIA. Cold climate location (Hokkaido) provides free cooling advantage.
notes: Listed on aidatacenterindex under NVIDIA. Liquid cooling not explicitly confirmed in search results but expected given NVIDIA Blackwell infrastructure requirements. Cold Hokkaido climate advantageous. Source: aidatacenterindex.com.

facility_name: Samsung Chemours/LiquidStack Immersion Cooling Test Facility
operator_company: Samsung Electronics / Chemours / LiquidStack / PKI Corporation
cooling_type: Two-Phase Immersion Cooling (Chemours Opteon fluid)
opportunity_summary: Samsung qualified Chemours Opteon as first two-phase immersion cooling fluid for Gen 4 SSDs. Commercial 48U tank test with LiquidStack and PKI Corp. Accelerates immersion cooling adoption for Samsung memory/logic products.
notes: Immersion cooling explicitly confirmed by Chemours press release (Aug 2025). Exact test facility location not disclosed - coordinates are placeholder (Seoul). Source: Chemours press; HPCwire; Cooling Post.

facility_name: TSMC Direct-to-Silicon Liquid Cooling R&D (CoWoS Platform)
facility_type: Advanced Packaging R&D with Integrated Cooling
cooling_type: Direct-to-Silicon Microfluidic Liquid Cooling (on CoWoS substrate)
opportunity_summary: TSMC demonstrated direct-to-silicon liquid cooling integrated on CoWoS platform at IEEE ECTC 2025. Cools 2kW die with 40°C water using <10W pump power. Could reduce DC cooling infrastructure by ~50%. Part of 3DFabric advanced packaging.
notes: This is R&D/demo, not a production data center. Represents next-gen chip-level cooling that would be deployed IN data centers. Confirmed at IEEE ECTC 2025. Source: SemiWiki; Tom's Hardware.

facility_name: Bosch/JetCool Liquid Cooling Investment (Venture Portfolio)
cooling_type: Direct-to-Chip Liquid Cooling (JetCool technology)
opportunity_summary: Bosch Ventures led JetCool's $17M Series A. JetCool develops microconvective liquid cooling for data centers. Bosch does not operate its own liquid-cooled DC, but has strategic interest in DC cooling via venture investment.
notes: Bosch does NOT operate a liquid-cooled data center. This entry documents Bosch's strategic DC cooling interest. Bosch operates semiconductor fabs in Dresden and Reutlingen but no confirmed liquid-cooled DC. Source: JetCool press.

facility_name: NorthC Eindhoven High Tech Campus Data Center
cooling_type: Liquid Cooling + Immersion Cooling capable
opportunity_summary: NorthC operates on Eindhoven High Tech Campus where ASML, NXP, Philips work alongside startups. Supports liquid and immersion cooling for HPC/AI workloads. ASML itself does not operate its own DC but uses this ecosystem.
notes: ASML does not publicly operate its own liquid-cooled data center. NorthC serves the semiconductor R&D ecosystem in Eindhoven/Veldhoven region. Liquid/immersion cooling confirmed by NorthC website. Source: NorthC Datacenters.

facility_name: NorthC Eindhoven 2 (High Tech Gateway) Data Center
cooling_type: Liquid Cooling + Immersion Cooling capable
opportunity_summary: Second NorthC facility near ASML HQ in Veldhoven. Advanced cooling including liquid and immersion cooling for AI/HPC simulations. Serves semiconductor design and EUV lithography simulation workloads.
notes: Liquid/immersion cooling confirmed by NorthC website. ASML HQ is in nearby Veldhoven. Source: NorthC Datacenters.

facility_name: Siemens-nVent AI Data Center Reference Architecture
cooling_type: Direct Liquid Cooling (nVent DLC + Siemens power/automation)
opportunity_summary: Siemens and nVent joint reference architecture for 100MW+ hyperscale AI DCs. Combines Siemens power distribution/energy management with nVent DLC and coolant distribution. Targets NVIDIA-based infrastructure. Siemens does NOT operate its own liquid-cooled DC.
notes: Siemens is a DC cooling VENDOR, not an operator of its own liquid-cooled DC. Their Greenergy showcase DC in Estonia uses AI-optimized air cooling (PUE 1.15), not liquid cooling. Siemens also invested $165M in US DC manufacturing. Source: Engineering.com; Siemens press.

facility_name: Samsung SDS Chuncheon Data Center
cooling_type: Natural Airflow Design (NOT liquid cooled)
opportunity_summary: Samsung SDS facility opened Sept 2019. 39,780 sqm, PUE 1.50. Uses natural airflow design, NOT liquid cooling. Included for completeness as a Samsung semiconductor ecosystem DC without liquid cooling.
notes: Explicitly NOT liquid cooled - uses natural airflow. PUE 1.50 is significantly worse than Dongtan's 1.12. Potential upgrade candidate for liquid cooling. Source: datacentermap.com.

facility_name: SK hynix / SK Group NVIDIA AI Factory (Icheon Campus)
operator_company: SK hynix / SK Group / SK Telecom
cooling_type: Liquid Cooling (for dense GPU server racks)
opportunity_summary: SK Group building NVIDIA-powered AI factory with 50,000+ GPUs. Initial deployment at SK hynix Icheon campus, expanding to Yongin Semiconductor Cluster. Liquid cooling for dense GPU racks. RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell initial 2,000 units. Operated by SK Telecom.
notes: Liquid cooling confirmed by NVIDIA/SK press releases. Platform supports semiconductor manufacturing AI, digital twins, robotics, LLM training. Will serve SK affiliates, government, and Korean startups. Source: NVIDIA Newsroom; SK eng.sk.com.

facility_name: Foxconn-SoftBank Lordstown Data Center Equipment Factory
facility_type: Data Center Equipment Manufacturing (incl. liquid cooling subsystems)
cooling_type: Manufacturing facility for liquid cooling equipment (CDUs, racks)
opportunity_summary: Former GM plant, acquired by SoftBank for $375M. First manufacturing site for $500B Stargate project (SoftBank/OpenAI/Oracle/MGX). Foxconn manufactures liquid cooling subsystems, server racks, HVAC, power distribution. Up to $3B SoftBank investment planned.
notes: This is a MANUFACTURING facility for DC equipment including liquid cooling, not a data center itself. Foxconn expanding liquid cooling production capacity for cloud/networking at California and Ohio sites. Source: DCD; ManufacturingDive; Tom's Hardware.

facility_name: Foxconn-SoftBank-ZutaCore Liquid Cooled AI Server (Reference Deployment)
facility_type: Two-Phase Direct Liquid Cooling AI Server
cooling_type: Two-Phase Direct Liquid Cooling (ZutaCore HyperCool, NVIDIA H200 GPUs)
opportunity_summary: First implementation of ZutaCore two-phase DLC with NVIDIA H200 GPUs. Rack-integrated solution by Foxconn. Deployed for SoftBank. Foxconn offers liquid-to-air sidecar and liquid-to-liquid CDU solutions up to 1300kW capacity.
notes: ZutaCore two-phase DLC confirmed by insideHPC (Feb 2025). Exact deployment location within SoftBank's infrastructure not specified - Tokyo is approximate. Foxconn also expanding liquid cooling subsystem production at GTC 2026. Source: insideHPC; Foxconn press; Digitimes.

facility_name: Foxconn Mount Pleasant Wisconsin AI Server Factory
cooling_type: Manufacturing facility includes liquid cooling assembly/testing
opportunity_summary: $569M expansion for OpenAI server rack manufacturing. Foxconn producing 1,000+ AI server racks/week. Multiple generations of AI data center racks with improved rack architecture for US manufacturing. Liquid cooling integration for Blackwell-based systems.
notes: OpenAI partnership confirmed by DCD and ConstructionDive. Liquid cooling assembly capability not explicitly confirmed for this specific site but Foxconn offers full liquid cooling solutions (CDUs up to 1300kW). Source: ConstructionDive; TrendForce; DCD.

facility_name: Foxconn Guadalajara Mexico NVIDIA Blackwell Server Factory
cooling_type: Manufacturing facility for liquid-cooled server assembly
opportunity_summary: World's largest NVIDIA Blackwell motherboard/server assembly plant. Planned capacity: 20,000 servers/month. Supports GB200 NVL72 liquid-cooled rack-scale systems. Key supply chain node for NVIDIA AI infrastructure.
notes: Manufacturing facility, not a data center. Assembles servers that require liquid cooling (GB200 NVL72 is inherently liquid-cooled). Liquid cooling assembly capability implied by product requirements. Source: Tom's Hardware; Digitimes.
